nodes.label: Lenia — Biology of Artificial Life
nodes.summary: Continuous CA, bell kernel + polynomial growth, 400+ solitons.

nodes.label: Lenia and Expanded Universe
nodes.summary: Multichannel + higher-D Lenia.

nodes.label: Existence of Life in Lenia
nodes.summary: Soliton stability & parameter-space invariance.

nodes.label: Glaberish: generalized composition rules
nodes.summary: Decouples kernel from growth; new families.

nodes.label: Flow-Lenia (mass-conservative)
nodes.authors: Plantec, Hamon, Etcheverry, Oudeyer, Moulin-Frier, Chan
nodes.summary: Mass-conserving Lenia with parameters localized in the field. Reproduced minimally in proto/flow_lenia.py — mass conserved to 4e-15 but Orbium params produce stationary rings, not gliders. Flow-Lenia is a different substrate, not a drop-in alternative for Pole 2.

nodes.label: flow_lenia.py port + smoke tests
nodes.summary: 145-LOC NumPy port of Plantec 2022's reintegration-tracking update. Orbium params → stationary ring solitons (mass conserved to 4e-15); two rings at sep ∈ {30, 50, 70, 90} px → no interaction. Confirms vanilla Lenia is primary substrate for Pole 2.

nodes.label: Lenia gliders are dissipative solitons (established lit)
nodes.summary: Mass conservation (Flow-Lenia) kills Lenia's translation because the dissipation gradient that drives motion is the source/sink term `G(K*A)`. This framing is STANDARD: Purwins/Bode-Liehr-Schenk dissipative-soliton lit (2002+), Wuensche 2018 (CA gliders = Prigogine analogs), Kojima 2023 (asymptotic Lenia ≡ RD system). Our contribution is systematic instruction-set measurement, not the conceptual framing.

nodes.label: Purwins — Dissipative Solitons in RD Systems (textbook)
nodes.summary: Canonical reference for dissipative solitons in reaction-diffusion systems. Classifies particle-like behaviors (generation, annihilation, reflection, scattering, bound states). Parent literature for our Pole-2 collision phenomenology.

nodes.label: Bode et al. — Dissipative Soliton Interactions (RD)
nodes.summary: Direct precedent for collision-outcome studies in 3-component RD. Same phenomenology (scattering, annihilation, generation, bound states) as we observe in vanilla Lenia.

nodes.label: Wuensche-Coxon — Pulsing CA Glider Dynamics
nodes.summary: Frames CA gliders as discrete Prigogine analogs. Connects to Wolfram class 4, edge-of-chaos, computation.

nodes.label: Davis — Non-Platonic Autopoiesis in Asymptotic Lenia
nodes.summary: Asymptotic Lenia glider competence is resolution-dependent. Cautionary finding: Pole-2 eval should measure outcome stability under resolution perturbations.

nodes.label: Glover — Continuous CA for Halting Unpredictability
nodes.summary: Evolves continuous CA for 'mobility + mean-cell-value conservation' — close to our 5-leg substrate gate. Re-discovers gliders in 17/17 Lenia CAs; 4 new evolved variants.

nodes.label: MaCE — General Mass-Conserving CA Dynamics
nodes.summary: Attachable mass-conservation rule that reportedly produces 'abundant solitons' in Lenia — suggests mass conservation can preserve translation if formulated differently from Flow-Lenia. Stretch-goal substrate for the campaign.

nodes.label: Instruction-set stability under resolution perturbation
nodes.summary: Davis 2024 shows asymptotic-Lenia gliders are resolution-dependent. Open question for Pole 2: are the collision-outcome classes (passthrough, spawn+1) stable under grid-resolution + dt halving/doubling? Test before /init Phase 2.2 freezes the eval.

nodes.label: Particle Lenia (energy formulation)
nodes.summary: Lenia recast as gradient descent on a global energy — differentiable end-to-end.

nodes.label: Lenia as a Reaction-Diffusion System
nodes.summary: Asymptotic Lenia ≡ reaction-diffusion PDE. Opens classical dynamical-systems tools.

nodes.label: The Glider Equation for Asymptotic Lenia
nodes.summary: Velocity-free PDE characterizing gliders; gradient descent on the equation discovers new gliders. Bridges Lenia ↔ neural-field models.

nodes.label: ASAL (Sakana AI)
nodes.authors: Kumar, Lu, Kirsch, Tang, Stanley, Isola, Ha
nodes.summary: CLIP-as-judge × 3 objectives × 7 ALife substrates.

nodes.label: IMGEP — Intrinsically Motivated Discovery in Lenia
nodes.summary: Curiosity-driven diversity search on Lenia, FM-free. ICLR 2020 — predates ASAL by 4 years.

nodes.label: Sensorimotor Agency in CA via Diversity Search
nodes.summary: Discovers Lenia creatures that navigate obstacle fields robustly. Operationalizes 'agency' as a perturbation-response diversity signal. Science Advances 2024.

nodes.label: Curiosity-driven AI Scientist on Flow-Lenia
nodes.summary: IMGEP + evolutionary activity / compression / multi-scale entropy on Flow-Lenia ecosystems.

nodes.label: Growing Neural Cellular Automata
nodes.summary: End-to-end differentiable CA. The canonical 'learnable Lenia'. 1×1 conv + ReLU. Grows + regenerates target images.

nodes.label: Self-classifying MNIST Digits (NCA)
nodes.summary: NCA classifies MNIST via local message-passing only — no global pooling. 22 k params.

nodes.label: A Path to Universal NCA
nodes.summary: Trains NCA to do matrix multiplication, matrix transpose, AND emulate a NN solving MNIST — all within the CA state. GECCO 2025.

nodes.label: NCA for ARC-AGI
nodes.summary: First NCA attack on an explicit symbolic-reasoning benchmark. ALife 2025.

nodes.label: Reservoir Computing using CA
nodes.summary: CA as reservoir for ESN-style computation; edge-of-chaos rules give high computational capacity.

nodes.label: Reservoir Computers at Edge of Chaos?
nodes.summary: Empirically tests EOC hypothesis for RC. The right physics framing for 'where in Lenia parameter space does life live?'

nodes.label: Amari Neural Field Equation
nodes.summary: ∂_t u = -u + ∫ w(x-y) f(u(y)) dy + I — structurally identical to asymptotic Lenia. Bump attractors = solitons.

nodes.label: Wilson-Cowan Population Dynamics
nodes.summary: Continuous-population neural dynamics with E + I channels — multichannel Lenia of the brain.

nodes.label: Orbium (canonical Lenia glider)
nodes.summary: Continuous analog of GoL glider. R=13, T=10, μ=0.15. Reproduced in lenia-baseline.

nodes.label: Sep-CMA-ES
nodes.summary: ASAL's optimizer for continuous targets + open-endedness in Lenia rule space.

nodes.label: CLIP embedding as ALife judge
nodes.summary: ASAL's core idea: turn qualitative 'is this lifelike?' into a differentiable scalar via CLIP text-image alignment / temporal novelty / embedding coverage.

nodes.label: lenia-baseline (this orbit)
nodes.summary: Reproduces Orbium with Chan parameters; FFT toroidal convolution; mass + COM tracking.

nodes.label: lenia-zoo (this orbit)
nodes.summary: 7 species × 5 diagnostics. 5-dim foundation-model-free vector (mass_cv, speed, footprint, dihedral_symmetry, temporal_complexity) cleanly separates translator/rotator/undulator + static-blob negative control.

nodes.label: dihedral_symmetry diagnostic
nodes.summary: Rotation-aware D_n score. Synorbium 0.31 → 0.78 after upgrade.

nodes.label: temporal_complexity diagnostic
nodes.summary: Pixel-wise std of COM-centered frames. Load-bearing leg against trivial winners (static blob = 0).

nodes.label: Lenia behavior modes (translate / rotate / undulate)
nodes.summary: Empirically observed in lenia-zoo. The natural axis along which a non-FM metric should be discriminative.

nodes.label: Eval-matrix sketch: 4-leg gate + scalarized score
nodes.summary: Gate: persistent ∧ mass_cv<0.05 ∧ temporal>τ ∧ dihedral>0.6. Score: monotone fn of speed, footprint, dihedral. In proto/lenia-zoo/report.md.

nodes.label: Lenia as a substrate for computation
nodes.summary: Two paths: (a) hand-build logic-gate collisions among solitons à la GoL-glider-guns; (b) train K, G end-to-end (NCA). Path (b) is empirically winning — Béna 2025 emulates a NN inside the CA state.

nodes.label: Lenia ≡ neural field (PDE limit)
nodes.summary: Asymptotic Lenia is reaction-diffusion → which is the Amari/Wilson-Cowan neural field equation with a custom kernel and pointwise nonlinearity. Solitons ≡ bump attractors. Math is shared.

nodes.label: Compositional soliton interactions
nodes.summary: Lenia gliders typically merge/annihilate on collision rather than acting as a logic gate. Open question: can a parameter regime (or a trained K, G) be found where collisions are deterministic AND function-shaped? PARTIALLY ANSWERED by orbit-lenia-collisions — 4/8 sample angles pass through, 2/8 spawn-3.

nodes.label: lenia-collisions (this orbit)
nodes.summary: Two-round Orbium and Synorbium collision sweep with delta-based classifier. Three-class instruction set (annihilate, passthrough, spawn+1) at 45°-resolution. Spawn+1 at 135° robust across 30-px impact-parameter window, transitions to merge−1 at b=+20. Synorbium reproduces the classes with shifted boundaries — instruction set is creature-portable.

nodes.label: Collision-outcome instruction set
nodes.summary: Map of (creature-A × creature-B × angle × phase × offset) → outcome-class. Vanilla Lenia already exhibits ≥3 classes (annihilate, pass-through, spawn-3). The substrate analog of a CPU's opcode table.

nodes.label: Information capacity of Lenia solitons
nodes.summary: Bits per glider, channel capacity for soliton-based communication. No measurement in the literature.

nodes.label: Diagnostic vector as auxiliary loss for trained NCA
nodes.summary: Does forcing a trained NCA to satisfy the 5-leg gate (persistent, mass_cv, temporal, dihedral) cost or buy task accuracy? Direct test of 'lifelikeness as an inductive bias'.

edges.label: uses Lenia as one of 7 substrates
trained CA for arithmetic + NN emulation
Lenia analog of classical RD soliton interactions
mobility + conservation criterion (similar to our 5-leg gate)

meta.intent_statement: Systematically map Lenia's collision-outcome instruction set as a basis for compositional analog computation. Empirical anchors: 3 outcome classes in vanilla Lenia at 45° resolution, robust over 30-px impact-parameter window, reproduced on Synorbium. Mass-conserving Flow-Lenia detour: Orbium params produce stationary rings, no inter-ring interaction. Literature recheck: dissipative-soliton framing is standard (Purwins, Wuensche, Glover, Davis); our novelty is systematic instruction-set measurement + computational framing. Ready to write proposed_eval.yaml.
